content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 20, 1992


Stacey Wilson
Friends of Tom Bane for Assembly
5430 Van Nuys Blvd., Suite 203
Van Nuys, CA 91401

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-584

Dear Ms. Wilson:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on
behalf of Assemblyman Tom Bane regarding the "personal use" of
campaign funds provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").¹

QUESTION

May Assemblyman Tom Bane use surplus campaign funds to rent
or lease real property for the purpose of storing desks,
computers, printers, and other campaign assets until these items
are sold or donated?

CONCLUSION

The rental or lease of real property for the purpose of
storing campaign assets is a campaign expense which is directly
related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.
Thus, Assemblyman Tom Bane may use campaign funds to rent or lease
real property for up to one year at a time to store campaign
equipment until these items are sold or donated for up to one
year. The rental or lease must be in the committee's name.

FACTS

Assemblyman Tom Bane is leaving office effective
November 30, 1992. He would like to use surplus campaign funds to
rent space for the purpose of storing desks, computers, printers,

_______________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-92-584
Page 2


and other campaign equipment while waiting for these items to be
sold or donated.

ANALYSIS

Section 89512 enunciates the standards applicable to the
expenditure of campaign funds under the Act. The general rule is
that any expenditure of campaign funds must be, at a minimum,
reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental
purpose.²

Section 89517 sets out specific rules regarding the
restrictions on the use of campaign funds³ for the rent or lease
of real property. Pursuant to subdivision (b) of Section 89517,
campaign funds may be used to lease real property for up to one
year at a time where the use of that property is directly related
to political, legislative, or governmental purposes. The rent or
lease of storage space for campaign assets until they are sold or
donated is directly related to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose. Therefore, the use of campaign funds for
this purpose for up to one year is permissible under Section
89517. The rental or lease agreement of the storage space may be
renewed thereafter.

Campaign funds cannot be used for payment or reimbursement
for the lease, or rent, of real property where the lessee or
sublessor is a candidate, elected officer, campaign treasurer, or
any individual with authority to approve the expenditure of
campaign funds, or member of his or her family. (Section
89517(a)). Therefore, the lease or rental agreement must be in
the campaign committee's name.

Subdivision (c) of Section 89517 also provides that real
property is deemed directly related to a political, legislative,
or governmental purpose, so long as its use for other purposes is
only incidental to its use for political, legislative, or
governmental purposes. Consequently, no other use is permitted of
the storage area leased by the campaign committee, unless such use


2 Where an expenditure confers a substantial personal benefit on
the candidate or officeholder, the expenditure must be directly
related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. A
substantial personal benefit is defined as a direct personal
benefit to a candidate, elected officer, or any individual or
individuals with authority to approve the expenditure of campaign
funds held by a committee of $100 or more. (Section 89511(b)(3).)

3 "Campaign funds" includes any contributions, cash, cash
equivalents, and other assets received or possessed by a committee
as defined by subdivision (a) of Section 82013. (Section
89511(b)(1).)

Our File No. A-92-584
Page 3


is incidental to its use for a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose.⁴

When campaign funds become surplus funds, the personal use
law further limits their disposition.⁵ "Surplus funds" are
defined in section 89519 as campaign funds under the control of
the former candidate or elected officer (1) upon leaving any
elected office, or (2) at the end of the post-election reporting
period following the defeat of a candidate for elective office.
Thus, on November 30, 1992, which is the date Assemblyman Bane
leaves office, the committee's campaign funds become surplus
campaign funds.

Once campaign funds become surplus, they may only be
expended for specific purposes. For example, section 89519(a)
permits the use of surplus campaign funds for the payment of
outstanding campaign debts or an elected officer's expenses. The
rent or lease of storage space for campaign assets is considered
an expenditure under Section 82025. Thus, Assemblyman Bane may
use surplus campaign funds to rent or lease space to store
campaign equipment.


I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested.⁶ If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Luisa Menchaca
Counsel, Legal Division

LM:docs:92584

Enclosures


⁴ Regulation 18951 (copy enclosed) provides guidance regarding
the incidental use of campaign funds.

⁵ For campaign funds raised prior to January 1, 1989, which are
not commingled with campaign funds listed after that date,
Elections Code section 12400 controls their disposition once they
become surplus.

⁶ Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
